a:r.a:t: Led multi-project management for the migration of critical IT operations services—including telephony, WAN, LAN, PAM/PKI, and telecommunication systems—to a new data center serving both national and international entities. Oversaw project delivery across multiple

, ensuring minimal service disruption and full compliance with operational and security standards. Held personnel responsibility for a team of over 100 staff members, coordinating cross-functional efforts between infrastructure, network, and security teams. The migration was executed within budget and timeline, enhancing infrastructure resilience and enabling future scalability for global operations
Led the expansion of the strategic crisis management and business continuity management (BCM) scope as part of the

+ and ABBA programs. Directed the design and rollout of advanced testing scenarios, including full-scale Black Building Exercises, to validate resilience, crisis response, and recovery capabilities under real-world conditions. Coordinated more than 200 stakeholders across multiple departments and geographic locations, ensuring alignment of processes, roles, and responsibilities. The program significantly improved
preparedness, reduced recovery times, and strengthened operational resilience for critical IT services
. Applied methods and standards: ITIL, ISO 22301, LCM, PRINCE2.

Developed and implemented comprehensive technical and operational documentation based on the OSI layer model beneath IT application level. This documentation framework provided a structured and consistent foundation for strategic crisis management, business continuity management (BCM), and IT service continuity management (ITSCM) initiatives. The approach improved cross-team transparency, facilitated faster incident resolution, and ensured that critical dependencies and interfaces were clearly identified and maintained. The deliverable became a central reference point for audits, regulatory compliance, and operational readiness
methods and standards: ITIL, ISO 27001, ISO 22301, LCM, PRINCE2.

Designed and implemented an end-to-end lifecycle management system covering strategic crisis management, business continuity management (BCM), and IT service continuity management (ITSCM). The framework
processes across planning, testing, maintenance, and continuous improvement, ensuring consistent application throughout the
. It provided full audit readiness for
and certification requirements, streamlined compliance reporting, and strengthened the
ability to maintain operational resilience under adverse conditions
methods and standards: ITIL, ISO 27001, ISO 22301, LCM, PRINCE2.

Served as Interim Service Manager for Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) and Privileged Access Management (PAM) during the transition of IT operations to an external service provider. Assumed full coordination responsibility for all service-related activities, ensuring operational stability and continuity throughout the outsourcing process. Successfully managed the ramp-up and transfer of new role ownership, establishing clear responsibilities, governance structures, and communication channels to support ongoing service delivery and compliance
methods and standards: ITIL, PMI, PRINCE2.
Provided strategic coaching to the department head during a major transformation of Windows patch planning
This initiative resolved critical operational process deficiencies, streamlined update cycles, and improved compliance with internal IT security standards. The
methods and standards: ITIL, PMI, PRINCE2.
